title: [Bug]: CI secret scan fails when ripgrep is unavailable
summary: The high-confidence secret scan depended on `rg`, which is available in the local release environment but was not available in the GitHub Actions runner.
observed: The post-release main CI security scan failed with `scripts/secret-scan.sh: 15: rg: not found`.
expected: The secret scan should prefer `rg` when available but fall back to `grep` so CI security checks remain portable.
reproduction: Run `scripts/secret-scan.sh` in a minimal environment where `grep` exists and `rg` is absent.
failing_test: Add a focused unit test that executes a copied scanner with a PATH containing only `grep`.
impact: Main branch CI can fail even when no secret material is present, reducing release confidence and obscuring real security findings.
delivery_semantics: The fix affects release and CI security validation only. It does not change NATS delivery, sink writes, idempotency, DLQ handling, or ACK ordering.
security: The fallback keeps the same high-confidence patterns and ignored paths. It does not lower the scanner's expected detection behavior.
acceptance: The scanner prefers `rg` when it is available.
The scanner falls back to `grep` when `rg` is unavailable.
The fallback excludes ignored build, site, distribution, local, and git paths.
A focused regression test covers the no-`rg` path.
The security scan passes in GitHub Actions.
tests: Run `pytest tests/unit/test_secret_scan_script.py -q`, `scripts/security.sh`, and the main CI workflow.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md so the next release notes explain the CI portability fix.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the portable secret scan has been published and CI evidence is available.
